channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Inspiring effort: De La Salle’s rally comes up short against Mater Dei in Open state title game
item.description: CIF Open Division state football championship 2024: De La Salle comes back from huge halftime deficit against Mater Dei, but big rally comes up short.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Sixers standout rookie Jared McCain is out indefinitely with a torn meniscus that requires surgery
item.description: Philadelphia 76ers rookie Jared McCain has a meniscus tear in his left knee that requires surgery and is expected to be out indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shiffrin recovering after abdominal surgery to clean out deep puncture wound suffered in race crash
item.description: BEAVER CREEK, Colo. (AP) — Mikaela Shiffrin is recovering after undergoing abdominal surgery on Thursday night to clean out a puncture wound she received in a giant slalom crash two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cornelia Huetter of Austria wins first-ever women's World Cup downhill held on Birds of Prey course
item.description: BEAVER CREEK, Colo. (AP) — Cornelia Huetter of Austria navigated the tricky and steep terrain Saturday to win the first-ever women's World Cup downhill held on the Birds of Prey course. Huetter finished in 1 minute, 32.38 seconds to hold off Italian ski racer Sofia Goggia by 0.16 seconds.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wild's Kirill Kaprizov scores off Flyers' goalie helmet, banking shot into net
item.description: Kirill Kaprizov doesn’t need much space to score a goal, but he’s not above getting a little help from the opposing goalie.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Football community rallies around NFL legend Randy Moss after revealing cancer diagnosis
item.description: The football community rallied around NFL legend Randy Moss after the former Vikings and Patriots wide receiver revealed he has been diagnosed with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dosunmu and Giddey lead the Bulls past the struggling Hornets 109-95
item.description: Josh Giddey had 17 points, nine rebounds and seven assists, and the Chicago Bulls beat the struggling Charlotte Hornets 109-95 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pro Football Hall of Famer Randy Moss reveals cancer diagnosis, 6-hour surgery
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Fame wide receiver Randy Moss said Friday he is being treated for cancer and is recovering from major surgery. In an appearance on Instagram Live, Moss, aided by a cane, introduced himself as a cancer survivor and thanked his “prayer warriors” for their support.
item.dc:creator: ZACH ALLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Soccer icon Alex Morgan makes waves for Rady Children’s Giveathon: how to help
item.description: This holiday season, former San Diego Wave FC star Alex Morgan is spreading some cheer by way of her foundation and support of the annual iHeart Rady Children’s Giveathon.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: SJ Earthquakes welcome 3 new players in deadline trade
item.description: (KRON) – Three players have been traded to the San Jose Earthquakes, the team announced Monday. Defender Dave Romney and midfielders Ian Harkes and Mark-Anthony Kaye will be making their way to Silicon Valley from the New England Revolution.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Joe Burrow's home reportedly broken in to Monday night during game
item.description: According to WLWT, Joe Burrow's Anderson Township, Ohio home was broken into and confirmed by the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Green Bay Packers sign Michael Jordan to their practice squad — the offensive lineman
item.description: The Green Bay Packers have signed Michael Jordan to their practice squad. But before Chicago sports fans bemoan the most legendary athlete in city history going to the city's most hated rival, relax: It's not THAT Michael Jordan. For one, he didn't play football. For another, he's 61 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Silver Pigskin Finalist starts at Quarterback for Iowa Hawkeyes
item.description: Most walk-on players in college football never get the opportunity to take the field, especially not at quarterback, and certainly not for a Power Four football program like the Iowa Hawkeyes. But La Jolla alum Jackson Stratton has done just that.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch 'Monday Night Football' in 'The Simpsons' universe
item.description: The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Juan Soto agrees to record $765 million, 15-year contract with Mets, AP source says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Star outfielder Juan Soto and the New York Mets agreed Sunday to a record $765 million, 15-year contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press. The deal could escalate to $805 million and is believed to be the largest pact in team sports history.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC beats Flyers to sweep East Coast road trip
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since the first week of the NHL season, the Utah Hockey Club has won back-to-back games, and they did it on back-to-back nights.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James misses 1st game this season, sidelined versus Portland due to left foot soreness
item.description: LeBron James will sit out Sunday's game against the Portland Trail Blazers due to left foot soreness, the NBA career scoring leader's first missed game of the season.
item.dc:creator: DOUG PADILLA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: College Football Playoff's first 12-team bracket is set with Oregon No. 1 and SMU in, Alabama out
item.description: The new, 12-team College Football Playoff brings with it a promise to be bigger, more exciting, more lucrative. Perfect or 100% fair? Well, nobody ever believed that.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks starting goaltender Petr Mrazek placed on injured reserve with groin injury
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Blackhawks placed goaltender Petr Mrazek on injured reserve on Sunday with a left groin injury. Mrazek was replaced by Arvid Soderblom in the first period of Saturday's 4-2 loss to Winnipeg. The 32-year-old Mrazek is 7-11-1 with a 2.83 goals-against average and .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
